title: Cross-chain root sync via EIP-1186 storage proofs

artifacts.description: Library implementing RLP decoding and Merkle-Patricia Trie (MPT) proof verification for EIP-1186 storage proofs. Exposes verifyStorageProof(bytes32 stateRoot, address account, bytes32 slot, bytes[] calldata accountProof, bytes[] calldata storageProof) returning the slot value.

Abstract interface for L1 block hash oracles across supported L2s. Implementations: ArbL1BlockOracle (wraps ArbSys.getL1BlockHash), PolygonL1BlockOracle (wraps bor/state-sync block hash feed). Returns (blockNumber, stateRoot) for a given L1 block.

Core cross-chain relay contract. Maintains a registry of (chainId => sourceRegistryAddress => IL1BlockOracle). Accepts relay submissions: given an L1 block reference, an account proof for the source IdentityRegistry, and a storage proof for humanRootHistory[root] or agentRootHistory[root], verifies trustlessly via StorageProofVerifier and writes to foreignRootExists[chainId][root] = true. Emits RootRelayed(chainId, root, relayer).

Extended IdentityRegistry with: (1) foreignRootExists(uint256 chainId, bytes32 root) mapping populated by RootRelay; (2) handshakeVerify overload accepting a sourceChainId param that checks foreignRootExists in addition to local roots; (3) onlyRootRelay modifier restricting foreignRootExists writes.

Owner-governed registry mapping chainId => (registryAddress, oracleAddress, storageSlotLayout). Stores packed slot indices for humanRootHistory and agentRootHistory mappings to support future registry upgrades without redeploying RootRelay.

Foundry fuzz + unit tests. Includes fixtures of real eth_getProof responses captured from Base mainnet for humanRootHistory slots. Tests MPT proof accept/reject, RLP edge cases, and wrong-stateRoot reverts.

Foundry fork tests (Base + Arbitrum + Polygon forks). Simulates a full relay cycle: insert a root on Base, capture eth_getProof output, submit to Arbitrum RootRelay, assert foreignRootExists is set. Also tests invalid proofs, stale block references, and unauthorized registry writes.

TypeScript relayer utility. Calls eth_getProof on source chain for (registryAddress, [humanRootHistorySlot, agentRootHistorySlot]). Encodes account + storage proof as ABI-ready bytes[]. Exports buildRelayCalldata(root, chainId, provider) for submission to RootRelay.

Deterministic slot computation for mapping(bytes32 => ...) at known storage positions. Derives keccak256(abi.encode(root, mappingSlot)) for humanRootHistory and agentRootHistory given the registry's current storage layout.

Protocol spec covering: threat model (relayer is untrusted), block reference freshness window (configurable staleness bound in L1 blocks), storage slot stability requirements, multi-chain deployment topology, and upgrade path if oracle contracts change.

Runbook for relayer operators: how to monitor new root insertions via RootInserted events on source chain, generate proofs using the SDK, estimate gas, handle reorgs, and configure the staleness window.

steps: Audit IdentityRegistry storage layout: pin exact slot indices for humanRootHistory and agentRootHistory mappings; document in ChainRegistry to ensure proof generation always targets the correct slot
Implement StorageProofVerifier library: RLP-decode account proof to extract storageRoot, then verify storage proof via MPT walk against storageRoot; return decoded slot value or revert on mismatch

Define IL1BlockOracle interface and implement ArbL1BlockOracle (ArbSys.getL1BlockHash) and PolygonL1BlockOracle (bor blockhash opcode / state-sync precompile) with a freshness check (revert if L1 block older than STALENESS_BLOCKS)
Implement ChainRegistry with owner-governed mapping of chainId to (registryAddress, oracleAddress, humanRootHistorySlot, agentRootHistorySlot) to decouple chain config from RootRelay logic

Implement RootRelay: validate source chain is registered, fetch stateRoot from oracle, call StorageProofVerifier, confirm slot value == keccak256(root) or nonzero sentinel, write foreignRootExists, emit RootRelayed

Extend IdentityRegistry: add foreignRootExists mapping with onlyRootRelay write guard; add handshakeVerify(bytes32 root, uint256 sourceChainId) that checks local roots OR foreignRootExists[sourceChainId][root]
Write Foundry unit tests for StorageProofVerifier using real eth_getProof fixtures from Base; cover valid proofs, tampered proofs, wrong account, and empty slot edge cases
Write Foundry fork tests for full relay cycle across Base→Arbitrum and Base→Polygon; assert correct state transitions and revert conditions (stale block, unregistered chain, bad proof)
Implement TypeScript SDK: slotLayout.ts for deterministic slot derivation; storageProof.ts wrapping eth_getProof and encoding output for RootRelay.submitProof ABI call
Deploy contracts on Arbitrum Sepolia and Polygon Amoy testnets; register chains in ChainRegistry; run SDK-driven relay of a test root end-to-end and verify foreignRootExists on destination
Produce cross-chain-root-sync.md spec covering threat model, staleness bounds, upgrade path, and multi-chain topology
Write relay-operator-guide.md covering event monitoring, proof generation, gas estimation, and reorg handling
dependencies: EIP-1186 eth_getProof RPC method available on source chain nodes
Arbitrum ArbSys precompile (0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000064) for L1 block hash access
Polygon bor blockhash opcode or state-sync precompile for L1 state root feed
MPT/RLP library (e.g., ethereum-optimism/optimism/packages/contracts-bedrock/src/libraries/trie or Hamdiakoguz/eth-proof port to Solidity)
Existing IdentityRegistry deployment on Base with stable storage layout
Foundry fork testing infrastructure with live RPC endpoints for Base, Arbitrum, and Polygon
